cot_prefix: Okay, so I need to figure out the order of the fruits from cheapest to most expensive based on the given information. Let me start by listing out all the fruits: loquats, peaches, pears, plums, and watermelons. The problem states that watermelons are the most expensive. So watermelon is definitely at the top of the price list. Then it says peaches are more expensive than loquats. So peaches > loquats. Then the plums are the second-cheapest. That means if we order them from cheapest to most expensive, plums are in the second position. And the pears are the third-most expensive. So if we order from cheapest to most expensive, the third from the top would be pears. Let me write this down step by step. Let me denote the fruits as L (loquats), P (peaches), Pe (pears), Pl (plums), and W (watermelons). The order from cheapest (1st) to most expensive (5th) is what we need to determine. Given: 1. Watermelons are the most expensive → W is 5th. 2. Peaches are more expensive than loquats → P > L. 3. Plums are the second-cheapest → Pl is 2nd. 4. Pears are the third-most expensive → Pe is 3rd.
